subject: ap-government
title: Argument Essay — Federal vs. State Responsibility for Environmental Policy
stimulus: Environmental regulation is a complex policy area that involves both the states and the federal government. Develop an argument that takes a position on whether the federal government should be primarily responsible for managing environmental policy or if it should be the responsibility of the states.

parts.prompt: Respond to the prompt with a defensible claim or thesis that establishes a line of reasoning. (Use at least one piece of evidence from one of the following foundational documents: Brutus 1, The Federalist 10, The Preamble of the United States Constitution.)
parts.scoring_notes: Thesis must take a clear position and establish a line of reasoning. Does NOT need foundational documents.

scoring_points.description: Row A — Thesis/Claim: Respond to the prompt with a defensible claim or thesis that establishes a line of reasoning.

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “State governments are better at making environmental policy because they are closer to the people.”

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “State governments can customize policy better because they act as laboratories of democracy.”

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “The federal government is better at making environmental policy because environment issues in one state can impact other states.”

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “The federal government is better at making environmental policy because policy needs to be the same nationwide.”

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “The federal government is better at making environmental policy because the federal government has more resources (financial or institution).”

wrong_examples: “There is a debate about whether the national or state governments should take the lead in environmental policy.” Do not respond to the prompt
“There are times when states do things that help their citizens out better than the national government.”
common_traps: Do NOT require foundational documents in thesis
A because clause with reasons IS a line of reasoning

parts.prompt: Support your claim with at least TWO pieces of specific and relevant evidence. One piece of evidence must come from one of the foundational documents listed. A second piece of evidence can come from any other foundational document or from course concepts.
parts.scoring_notes: TIERED SCORING: Award HIGHEST tier met. Each tier 1pt progressive. Tier 2+ requires Row A.

scoring_points.description: Evidence Tier 1: One relevant piece of evidence.

required_elements: One relevant evidence

scoring_points.description: Evidence Tier 2: One specific piece supporting thesis OR two relevant. Requires Row A.

required_elements: One specific piece supporting thesis OR two relevant
Row A earned
alternatives.correct_example: Uses evidence to directly support thesis.

scoring_points.description: Evidence Tier 3: Two specific pieces supporting thesis, one from listed foundational document. Requires Row A.

required_elements: Two specific pieces
One from listed foundational document
Row A earned
alternatives.correct_example: Uses foundational doc + concept supporting thesis.

parts.prompt: Use reasoning to explain why your evidence supports your claim or thesis.
parts.scoring_notes: Must have earned Row A and at least 2 points in Row B. Listing evidence without explaining the connection to the claim does not earn this point.

scoring_points.description: Row C — Reasoning: Use reasoning (classification, process, causation, or comparison) to explain how or why the evidence supports the claim or thesis.

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “Brutus I would support the idea that states should be in charge of environmental policy because too much federal power will curtail liberty/fail to address local concerns.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “Because the environment is central to ‘promoting general welfare’ through the health and well-being of citizens, the federal government should address issues such as the environment.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “Competing interests within a large republic require the federal government to mediate between these interests because this competition can result in improvement to policy overall.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The federal government can make treaties on environmental issues. As environment policy requires international cooperation, the federal government should have the authority over environmental policy.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The necessary and proper clause allows the federal government to take action, making policy uniform and expedient.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “Policy diffusion/laboratories of democracy allow state innovation on the environment to spread to other states or the federal government.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The supremacy clause establishes that federal laws and treaties made in line with the Constitution constitute the ‘supreme law of the land’ and take priority over conflicting state laws.”

common_traps: Must have earned Row A and at least 2 points in Row B
Simply restating evidence without reasoning does not earn this point

parts.prompt: Respond to an opposing or alternate perspective using refutation, concession, or rebuttal.
parts.scoring_notes: Must have earned Row A. Restating the opposite of the thesis does not earn the point. Must describe an alternate perspective AND engage with it (not just acknowledge it).

scoring_points.description: Row D — Responds to Alternate Perspectives: Respond to an opposing or alternate perspective using rebuttal or refutation.

required_elements: Describes alternate perspective AND rebuts/refutes it
alternatives.correct_example: “Some people say that the federal government is better, but federal policies are ‘one size fits all’ and do not always meet local needs.”

required_elements: Describes alternate perspective AND rebuts/refutes it
alternatives.correct_example: “While some argue that states should control environmental policy, the federal government has better resources/mediates competition to improve policy/can interact with foreign nations on the issue.”

wrong_examples: “Some argue that states should control environmental policy.” Describe an alternate perspective but do not refute, concede, or rebut that perspective
“Some people say that the federal government is better in developing environmental policy.”
common_traps: Simply identifying an alternate perspective without rebutting/refuting it does not earn this point
